---
name: instructional-analysis
description: "Learner and context analysis, needs assessment, task decomposition, and constraints gathering for developer education projects."
---

# Instructional Analysis

Purpose: run rapid, evidence-driven analysis to define who the learners are, what they need to do, and the constraints that affect design.

When to use: at project kickoff, before writing objectives or content.

Core activities:
- Learner profile: prior knowledge, roles, motivation, time availability.
- Needs assessment: performance gap, business outcomes, KPIs.
- Task analysis: break complex skills into teachable sub-skills.
- Constraints inventory: tech, budget, accessibility, localization.

Templates
- Learner profile fields: role, experience (yrs), common tools, pain points, availability.
- Needs statement: "Current: X. Desired: Y. Gap: Z. Success metric: M."

## Templates

Learner profile (CSV):

Role,ExperienceYears,CommonTools,PainPoints,Availability
Backend Engineer,3,Docker;Node;Git,Hard to debug CI failures,2 hours/week

Needs statement (one-line):
"Current: Engineers frequently fail integration tests. Desired: 90% pass rate on CI within 2 runs. Gap: missing testing knowledge and flaky infra. Success metric: CI pass rate >=90% within 2 runs."
